# host_mac_settings (Resource)

Manages multiple macOS settings backed by `defaults` keys as one grouped Terraform resource.

Use this resource when you want one compact macOS settings block. Put related preferences in `groups` when they share a domain, or use `settings` for one-off entries. Each setting maps to one macOS preference identified by a defaults domain, key, and scope.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "host_mac_settings" "settings" {
  groups = {
    dock = {
      domain = {
        apple = "dock"
      }

      settings = {
        autohide = {
          key   = "autohide"
          value = true
        }

        hide_recent_apps = {
          key   = "show-recents"
          value = false
        }
      }
    }

    global = {
      domain = {
        global = true
      }

      settings = {
        languages = {
          key   = "AppleLanguages"
          value = ["ko-KR", "en-US"]
        }
      }
    }

    battery = {
      domain = {
        apple = "menuextra.battery"
      }

      restart = ["SystemUIServer"]

      settings = {
        show_percent = {
          key   = "ShowPercent"
          value = "YES"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Value Attributes

Set `value` to a bool, number, string, or list of strings. In `groups`, each setting only needs `key` and `value` because the domain is inherited from the group.

## Restart Behavior

The provider writes all configured settings, collects restart processes, de-duplicates them, and restarts each process once. When `restart` is omitted for an entry or group, the provider applies built-in restarts for known domains such as Dock, Finder, SystemUIServer menu extras, global preferences, trackpad preferences, and accessibility preferences. Set `restart = []` to disable restarts.

## Destroy Behavior

Removing an entry from `settings` or `groups`, or destroying the resource, leaves that macOS setting in place by default. Set `delete_on_destroy = true` on an entry or group when Terraform should delete defaults keys after removal or destroy.

## Import

Import selected existing settings by naming each map entry and pointing it at a defaults key:

```shell
terraform import 'host_mac_settings.settings' 'dock_autohide=user:com.apple.dock:autohide,dock_hide_recent_apps=user:com.apple.dock:show-recents'
```

Each import entry uses `<map_key>=<domain>:<key>`, `<map_key>=user:<domain>:<key>`, or `<map_key>=currentHost:<domain>:<key>`. The provider reads only the defaults keys listed in the import ID; it does not scan an entire defaults domain.

Use `<group>.<setting>=<default_id>` when importing directly into `groups`:

```shell
terraform import 'host_mac_settings.settings' 'clock.analog=user:com.apple.menuextra.clock:IsAnalog,clock.show_ampm=user:com.apple.menuextra.clock:ShowAMPM'
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `groups` (Dynamic) Named groups of macOS settings that share `domain`, `current_host`, `delete_on_destroy`, and `restart`. Each group has `domain` and `settings`; each nested setting only needs `key` and `value`.
- `settings` (Dynamic) Named macOS settings to manage. Each map value is an object with `domain`, `key`, and `value`, plus optional `current_host`, `delete_on_destroy`, and `restart`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Resource identifier, always `mac_settings`.
